LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS OFF CALENDAR AT THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-18-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAP, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. THE ISSUE RAISED IS EXPOSURE AND DEFENDANT HAS TESTIFIED TO THE USE OF DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS. THE REED DECLARATION GENERALLY DISCUSSES DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS AND ASSERTS MANY DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS BUT THAT DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PROVE THE DAP PRODUCTS HE USED CONTAINED ASBESTOS TO WHICH PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED. THE REED DECLARATION LACKS THE REQUISITE PERSUASIVENESS FOR IT CONTAINS VIRTUALLY NO FOUNDATIONAL FACTS FOR THE STATEMENTS MADE. PLAINTIFF ALSO PRESENTS EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON THE DECLARATIONS OF TREFRY, DUDICK AND THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF TREFRY. THE DICOLA OBJECTION IS OVERRULED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Goldstein of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Anthony Chiosso of Jackson & Wallace (982.6300) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAP, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. The issue raised is exposure and defendant has testified to the use of defendant's products. The Reed Declaration generally discusses defendant's products and asserts many did not contain asbestos but that does not demonstrate that plaintiff is unable to prove the DAP products he used contained asbestos to which plaintiff was exposed. The Reed Declaration lacks the requisite persuasiveness for it contains virtually no foundational facts for the statements made. Plaintiff also presents evidence creating a triable issue of fact based on the Declarations of Trefry, Dudick and the deposition testimony of Trefry. The DiCola objection is overruled. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
